codeReview.summary: The fix correctly filters out high-frequency global organizations and adds a title-similarity gate, but it still over-collects story provenance once any one cross-country title pair matches. Entity-derived geopolitical arcs can therefore still open with unrelated stories in the drilldown, so the feature does not fully satisfy the quick-fix contract.

issues.description: After the Jaccard gate succeeds for any one occurrence pair, `buildCountryCoOccurrences()` concatenates `const allOccs = [...occsA, ...occsB]` and adds every event ID from both countries into the arc. This still leaks unrelated stories into `storyIds` whenever the same entity appears in multiple stories per country. A direct check with the current code using two US Pentagon stories (one Iran-related, one unrelated budget hearing) plus one Iran Pentagon story produces `storyIds: ["us-related", "us-unrelated", "ir-related"]`, so the unrelated budget story would still appear in the geopolitical arc drilldown and violate VAL-QFIX-003's requirement that arc stories stay contextually relevant to the country pair.
issues.suggestedFix: Track which specific cross-country occurrence pairs clear the title-similarity threshold and add only those matched event IDs to the pair's `storyIds` and count calculations. Do not bulk-append every occurrence from both countries after a single positive match.

summary: Fail. High-frequency entities are filtered, but entity-derived arcs can still include unrelated stories because the matched-pair gate widens back out to all occurrences from both countries.
